title: with_ecc_key closure guarantees transient key flush
description: Device::with_ecc_key takes a closure and always calls key.flush() after the closure returns, whether it succeeded or failed. TPM transient object memory is a scarce resource (the TPM typically has only a handful of slots). If callers were given a raw EccKey and dropped it without flushing, the TPM slot would leak until reboot. The closure pattern makes it structurally impossible to forget: the key is created, used, and flushed atomically from the caller's perspective. The Drop impl on EccKey provides a second line of defense. Alternative rejected: returning EccKey directly to callers would require them to remember to call flush(), which is error-prone especially on error paths.
